response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior engineer with genuine low-latency and high-throughput systems experience, but he falls well short of this role's core minimum qualification: roughly seven months of professional C++ at National Instruments versus the required 8 years, with his systems work primarily in DLang. His infrastructure experience (micro-services at millions of requests/second, Kubernetes deployment of his own platform) is real but brief relative to the 5-year bar, and his leadership is expressed through workshops and mentoring rather than owning technical strategy for a cloud compute platform. He is a strong candidate for a senior full-stack or backend role, but not a fit for this C++-centric cloud platform tech lead position.
strengths: Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong testing and quality culture: TDD, open-source test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and delivered multi-day training courses
Broad polyglot range across backend, web, mobile, and embedded, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform on Kubernetes
concerns: Professional C++ experience is roughly 7 months, far below the 8-year minimum; his systems language of choice is D, not C++
Limited depth in large-scale cloud compute/infrastructure architecture ownership; recent roles are product-focused full-stack TypeScript work
No formal technical-leadership track record leading cloud or DevOps engineers or setting cross-team technical strategy

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud-platform technical leadership experience
